_description: Campaign Condition Table is 10-slot persistent. When a blank slot is rolled on the Campaign Condition Table (D100 → slot), generate a new condition from this Master table.

entries.name: Visibility limits
entries.description: Your vision is limited due to impenetrable darkness, fog, psionic interference, or visual distortions. This seems to defeat all conventional technological solutions.

entries.name: Variable by round
entries.description: Roll 1D6+8 at the beginning of the game and again at the end of each battle round to determine the visibility limit in inches next round.

entries.name: Fixed
entries.description: The visibility limit is 1D6+8 inches. Roll once to determine what it will be in every instance this condition applies.

entries.name: Variable by battle
entries.description: When setting up a battle, roll 1D6+8 to determine the visibility limit for this battle.

entries.name: Shooting penalties
entries.description: Your ability to track and shoot targets is affected by a natural phenomenon such as psionic disturbances, malfunction due to background signals, clouds of gas, or similar. A -1 penalty applies to all ranged attacks in certain circumstances.

entries.name: Random by round
entries.description: Each battle round roll 1D6; on 5-6, the penalty applies that round.

entries.name: Range
entries.description: All attacks over 15 inch range are penalized.

entries.name: Certain terrain types
entries.description: When setting up, randomly pick a terrain feature. All features of the same broad type (plants, rocks, water, etc.) apply the penalty to any shot that originates from, targets inside, or passes through the feature.

entries.name: Movement penalty
entries.description: Due to gravity distortion, slippery surfaces, crumbling ground material, or grasping plants, you are unable to Dash under certain circumstances.

entries.name: Table surface
entries.description: Anywhere on the table surface that is not a terrain feature. Figures in or on terrain features are not affected.

entries.name: Terrain features
entries.description: Anywhere that is in or on a terrain feature.

entries.name: Climbing
entries.description: While climbing up or down things.

entries.name: Crossing obstacles
entries.description: When crossing any kind of obstacle.

entries.name: Uncertain terrain features
entries.description: Long range scans are particularly unreliable here. If you use the Uncertain Terrain Features optional rule, add 1 additional Uncertain feature. If you do not use this rule, this result is instead treated as Unstable Terrain.

entries.name: Unstable terrain
entries.description: Many seemingly solid features seem extremely unstable, and the slightest noise could collapse them. When setting up, randomly select a terrain feature. All features of the same general type (rock, plant, etc.) are unstable. Each time a figure moves adjacent to or on the feature, or is fired upon while on the feature, roll 1D6. On a 1, the feature collapses and is removed from the table. Any figures in or on the feature will be knocked Sprawling.

entries.name: Shifting terrain
entries.description: At the end of each battle round, randomly select a terrain feature and then move it 1D6 inches in a random direction. Features can move off the table, in which case they are simply gone. Figures in or on the feature move with it but must each roll 4+ on 1D6 or be knocked Sprawling. Features will stop if they would otherwise collide with each other.

entries.name: Hazardous environment
entries.description: Randomly select two terrain features which become Impassable.

entries.name: Resource rich
entries.description: If you win the mission, roll one additional time on the Post Mission Find table. This roll CAN be a duplicate of a result you obtained from any rolls normally awarded by a mission.

entries.name: Heavy scanner signals
entries.description: If the mission uses Contacts, place one additional Contact marker initially. This has no other effect.

entries.name: Unusual activity levels
entries.description: The area is particularly hostile or relaxed. If using Contacts, modify the Aggression die.

entries.name: Increased activity
entries.description: Modify the Aggression die by +1 when testing for Contacts.

entries.name: Decreased activity
entries.description: Modify the Aggression die by -1 when testing for Contacts.

entries.name: Enemy activity
entries.description: The area is heavily patrolled, or the enemy is not here in great numbers. This affects the enemy number encountered when fighting Tactical Enemies.

entries.name: Reduced numbers
entries.description: Encounter sizes reduced by 1.

entries.name: Increased numbers
entries.description: Encounter sizes increased by 1.

entries.name: Drifting clouds
entries.description: Great clouds of gas, vapor, or pollen drift through the area. When setting up the battle, place three cloud markers 6 inches from the center of the table in random directions. Each produces a 2 inch radius cloud. These clouds cannot be fired through. Figures within a cloud can fire and be fired upon but it counts as cover. At the end of each round, roll 1D6 inches and a random direction. Every cloud drifts the same distance and direction.

entries.name: Safe
entries.description: The clouds interfere with visibility, but are not dangerous.

entries.name: Toxic
entries.description: Each battle, roll two D6 and pick the highest of the results to determine the toxin level. Any non-Lifeform figure that makes base contact with a toxic cloud at any point must roll 1D6 and on a roll equal to or above the toxin level, they become a Casualty or lose 1 KP.

entries.name: Corrosive
entries.description: Anyone that activates within a cloud takes a hit with +0 to the damage roll.

entries.name: Clear escape paths
entries.description: Once per battle round, you may select any one character that escapes the battle just as if they had moved off a battlefield edge.

entries.name: Confined spaces
entries.description: Randomly select two points on the battlefield edge when you set up terrain. The table can only be entered or exited at these two points.

entries.name: No Conditions
entries.description: All clear! No special conditions apply.
